curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: It's quite valuable to have the courage and the confidence to say, 'No, fuck off, leave me alone, thank you very much.'
quotes.speaker: Helen Mirren
quotes.context: In a 2013 Daily Mail interview, the Oscar-winning actress said that if she'd had a daughter, "fuck off" is the first phrase she would have taught her, to give her the confidence to set boundaries.

quotes.text: People ask why I always wore the sunglasses indoors. Honestly? Half the time it was because the lights were too bright. The other half it was so nobody could tell I was checking out whoever just walked through the door. I'm a gentleman, not a saint.
quotes.speaker: Dean Martin
quotes.context: Behind-the-scenes footage from the taping of 'The Dean Martin Show', NBC Studios, Burbank, California, Season 6, 1971, later included in the 2009 archive documentary reel
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the smooth deflection into a joke at his own expense is very Dino, and the 'gentleman, not a saint' line lands in his cadence. The giveaway is that Martin almost never explained himself even in jest; his whole persona was studied nonchalance, not this degree of self-commentary.

quotes.text: The rider said no brown M&Ms, which everyone takes the piss out of, but let me tell you something — the one time the caterers actually read the whole rider, everything else was perfect too. The one time they didn't? Freddie nearly went through a stage riser that wasn't bolted down. So yeah. Brown M&Ms.
quotes.speaker: Freddie Mercury
quotes.context: Backstage interview recorded for the BBC2 music documentary 'Rock Around the Clock', filmed at Wembley Arena, London, December 1981
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the M&M rider anecdote is famously associated with Van Halen, not Queen, which is exactly why attributing a version of it to Freddie is a clever misdirect. The giveaway is that detail-obsessed equipment safety reasoning, which sounds more like a tour manager talking than Mercury's more theatrical interview style.

quotes.text: Look, I've played arenas, stadiums, a shed in fucking Wolverhampton that smelled like wet dog and chips — and I will tell you right now, the shed was the best gig. Place was on fire. I nearly lost a finger in the barrier. Brilliant.
quotes.speaker: Lemmy Kilmister
quotes.context: Interview with Kerrang! magazine, published in the April 2008 issue, conducted backstage at the Brixton Academy, London
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the Wolverhampton specificity and the chips detail are pure Lemmy: equal reverence for the grimy small venue as the arena, plus cheerful near-injury. The subtle giveaway is 'I nearly lost a finger in the barrier,' which is a little too anecdote-complete to be spontaneous.

quotes.text: A couple nights ago, I was licking jelly off my boyfriend's penis. And I thought, "Oh my God — I'm turning into my mother!"
quotes.speaker: Sarah Silverman
quotes.context: From her 2005 stand-up special "Sarah Silverman: Jesus Is Magic." Her signature sweet-faced delivery lands a graphic sex image as the setup, with the punchline being the realization she's inheriting her mother's habits.

quotes.text: Over the past forty years I've been loaded on booze, coke, acid, Quaaludes, glue, cough mixture, heroin, Rohypnol, Klonopin, Vicodin, and too many other heavy-duty substances to list in this footnote. On more than a few occasions I was on all of these at the same time.
quotes.speaker: Ozzy Osbourne
quotes.context: From a footnote in his 2010 autobiography 'I Am Ozzy' (with Chris Ayres), cataloguing four decades of his own substance abuse.
